proof_authority: Lean source checked by the pinned Lean kernel

release_provenance.meaning: This checkout is a deliberately curated, independently verifiable public output of a larger ongoing formal-mathematics workflow. No public claim depends on private or unreleased work.
release_provenance.boundary: The workflow provenance explains the release discipline; it does not imply hidden proof authority or equivalence with private work.

status_taxonomy.proved here: Lean theorem in the committed formal-source checkpoint; no novelty assertion is implied
status_taxonomy.formalised here: Known mathematics represented and checked in Lean
status_taxonomy.unconditional progress: Genuine theorem that does not settle the open problem
status_taxonomy.conditional reduction: Exact implication whose remaining hypothesis is open
status_taxonomy.verified finite instance: Kernel-checked computation at a bounded range
status_taxonomy.cited only: External theorem used for positioning, not formalised

remaining_open_propositions.statement: Prove that S = ∑ φ(n)/2ⁿ is irrational, with no bound on a possible rational denominator.
Produce certified non-integrality witnesses at unbounded parameters. A sufficient one-parameter form is to produce them along the lcm diagonal.
Decide whether 1/2 lies in the Mersenne achievement set. Yes refutes universal #257; no closes only this route.
Prove irrationality for every infinite support A, not only the named support families formalised here.

non_claims.meaning: This release does not settle the universal Erdős #257 problem
This release does not settle Erdős #249
This release does not itself constitute a peer-reviewed publication record
This release is not asserted to be equivalent to any private development
Provider or model output is not proof authority; only the Lean kernel check is
No unpublished proof sketch or hidden artefact carries proof authority for this release

principal_claims.statement: For every integer b ≥ 2, the series ∑_{n≥1} 1/(bⁿ − 1) is irrational (Erdős, 1948).
E = ∑_{n≥1} 1/(2ⁿ − 1) is irrational, as the base-2 corollary of the full-support theorem.

For every base b ≥ 2, ∑_{n∈A} 1/(bⁿ − 1) is irrational for factorial support, power-of-two support, multiples, pairwise-coprime supports with summable reciprocals, eventually-periodic supports, residue classes, and the odd numbers. Proved families, not the universal statement.

S = ∑ φ(n)/2ⁿ is not equal to p/q for any integer p and any 1 ≤ q ≤ 79 639 646 646 701 375 323 355 774 875 831 053 (≈ 7.96 × 10³⁴). A lower bound on any rational representation, not a proof of irrationality.

S is irrational if and only if certified non-integrality witnesses occur at unbounded parameters. The pointwise completeness theorem and the diagonal supply theorem together show that an unbounded lcm-diagonal supply is equivalent as well. Producing that supply remains open.

At fixed parameters, a certified kill exists exactly when the corresponding tail difference is non-integral. At every positive shift, integrality of one tail difference forces S to be rational; thus irrationality is equivalent to pointwise non-integrality, and to pointwise finite certificates.
Kernel-checked certified kills for small periods and at 28 explicit lcm-diagonal scales through t = 64, plus finite certificate shards, each discharged by decide.

At each lcm-diagonal scale, integrality is equivalent to a foreign-defect term landing in an explicit full-target interval. Globally, S is irrational if and only if the full target is avoided at arbitrarily large diagonal scales; that cofinal avoidance is not proved.

If H divides the reduced denominator of a rational x and the reduced denominator of c·x divides H for an integer c, then the complementary factor x.den/H divides |c|. Scalar localisation transfers, rather than discards, that denominator information; this local obstruction is not an irrationality criterion.

A rational support-series value forces every zero window in the divisor-count coefficient to have length at most ε·log₂(N+1) + O_{ε,c,v}(1), uniformly in the support. This constrains coverage; it does not solve universal #257.

The Mersenne achievement set is compact, perfect, totally disconnected, nowhere dense, and has Lebesgue measure one. Membership is equivalent to greedy survival at every level; for the target 1/2 it is exactly equivalent to the canonical greedy orbit omitting infinitely many exponents. No finite normalized support has value 1/2, so positive membership would automatically produce an infinite rational-valued support and refute universal #257. Finite exact-rational death certificates soundly prove non-membership (3/4 is excluded at level one). The strict-tail Cantor geometry is recorded by Kovač–Tao; no novelty claim is made for the formalised refinements.

mathematical_programmes.title: Achievement-set geometry and the half-value seam
mathematical_programmes.mathematical_focus: Greedy coding, exact achievement-set geometry, dyadic skip bands, seam classification, and the last-producer tail residual for the target 1/2.
mathematical_programmes.claim_ceiling: Exact geometry and reductions for the half-value branch; neither half-membership nor universal Erdős #257 is proved.

mathematical_programmes.title: #249 certificate and harmonic interface
mathematical_programmes.mathematical_focus: Tail-difference certificates, exact completeness at fixed parameters, bounded lcm-diagonal instances, first-harmonic consumers, and the exact four-term pivot budget.
mathematical_programmes.claim_ceiling: The certificate formulation is exact and finite instances are checked; the unbounded certificate supply and irrationality of S remain open.

mathematical_programmes.title: Classical and structured support irrationality
mathematical_programmes.mathematical_focus: Classical full support, structured infinite supports, periodic nonnegative weights, signed periodic obstructions, and orthogonal-petal support combinatorics.
mathematical_programmes.claim_ceiling: These are structured families, coefficient classes, and a conditional bouquet reduction; arbitrary infinite supports in universal Erdős #257 remain open.

mathematical_programmes.title: #249 diagonal arithmetic and fresh-loss interfaces
mathematical_programmes.mathematical_focus: Exact interval avoidance on the lcm diagonal, fresh-prime endpoint defects, binary suffix arithmetic, affine escape sockets, and finite residue projections.
mathematical_programmes.claim_ceiling: Each interface sharpens or re-expresses the same supply problem; no unbounded avoidance, slack, or separated-projection supply is proved.

mathematical_programmes.title: Boolean Möbius carry and global rationality constraints
mathematical_programmes.mathematical_focus: Tempered binary carry orbits, Boolean Möbius reconstruction, reciprocal mass, unbounded carry states, and sublogarithmic divisor-coverage windows.
mathematical_programmes.claim_ceiling: These are exact characterisations and necessary consequences of rationality; they do not contradict every hypothetical rational value or settle universal Erdős #257.

mathematical_programmes.title: Transport, curvature, phase separation, and no-go results
mathematical_programmes.mathematical_focus: Finite differences of totient tails, exponent transport, affine moment annihilation, the exact first-harmonic pivot budget, harmonic phases, reconstruction defects, and countermodels to bounded local signatures.
mathematical_programmes.claim_ceiling: The consumers and countermodels are exact, but no cofinal curvature, transport, phase-separation, or anti-reconstruction producer is proved.

mathematical_programmes.title: Mersenne–Lambert identities and obstruction interfaces
mathematical_programmes.mathematical_focus: Dirichlet convolution identities, the Möbius-square lens, primitive-coordinate finite jets, exact tail atoms, sign-support no-go results, and conditional carry-rank growth.
mathematical_programmes.claim_ceiling: These are exact identities, obstruction theorems, and one conditional rank consequence; they do not form an irrationality criterion for S.

mathematical_programmes.title: Coprimality probability, gcd moments, and Stern–Brocot geometry
mathematical_programmes.mathematical_focus: Fair-coin gcd distributions, squared-Mersenne Lambert transforms, Pillai's gcd-sum function, Stern–Brocot cylinder measures, and Fibonacci/continuant run stability.
mathematical_programmes.claim_ceiling: Exact identities, denominator transfer, convergence, and combinatorial stability only; no irrationality or novelty claim.

mathematical_programmes.title: Half-carry compactness, windows, seams, and finite propagation
mathematical_programmes.mathematical_focus: Exact dyadic prefix coordinates, compactness from finite half-carry words, protected seams, inverse-parent contraction, suffix-cylinder propagation, and checked stages through depth 52.
mathematical_programmes.claim_ceiling: The closure implications, local propagation laws, and bounded stages are exact; no cofinal or coherent unbounded window family is proved, so neither half-membership nor universal Erdős #257 follows.

mathematical_programmes.title: Denominator, CRT, moment, and gauge obstructions
mathematical_programmes.mathematical_focus: Squared-Mersenne tail enclosures, exact reduced denominators, adelic divisibility, square-CRT correction removal, dyadic determinant substrate, certificate dimensions, and residual-gauge no-go results.
mathematical_programmes.claim_ceiling: These are exact arithmetic interfaces, finite mechanisms, and obstruction theorems; they do not supply an unbounded certificate family or an irrationality criterion for the totient constant.

current_priority.defect_class: Appendix A.1 introduces an unsafe interval, a dyadically unsafe skip, and a cleared band without first saying which tail or survival condition is being violated, so the exact interval theorem is harder to interpret than its algebra requires
current_priority.statement: Define the unsafe-band language in reader-facing terms and explain the reciprocal coordinate R before using the interval, while preserving the exact if-and-only-if, the width formula, the integral-reciprocal exclusion, the local-only boundary, and the fact that the band is not identified with transition cells -2 and -1.

reading_routes.intent: Understand what this repository proves, reduces, cites, leaves open, and how its claims are partitioned.
Browse bounded claim handles by exact registry status.
Trace one mathematical claim from exposition to checked source.
See how the #249 reduction, #257 families, carry trunk, and open targets relate.
Follow the proved #257 half-value spine from unique greedy coding to the exact last-transition tail residual, without treating local dyadic-safety bounds as a solution.

reading_routes.title: Mersenne–Lambert identities and obstruction interfaces

reading_routes.intent: Follow the fair-coin coprimality form of S and its denominator exclusion, squared-Lambert gcd moments, exact Stern–Brocot cylinder convergence, and Fibonacci/continuant stability of induced runs.
reading_routes.title: Coprimality probability, gcd moments, and Stern–Brocot geometry

reading_routes.intent: Follow the exact compactness implications from cofinal finite half-carry witnesses to half-membership, and the rewind, seam, suffix-cylinder, and finite-stage results that isolate the missing uniform construction.

reading_routes.intent: Follow exact arithmetic enclosures and denominator lower bounds, finite square-CRT suppression, dyadic moment and certificate interfaces, and gauge countermodels that delimit several plausible #249 routes.

reading_routes.intent: Understand the cited mathematical sources, their precise relationship to the release, and the priority boundary.
Understand the mathematical method, the evidence required for claim transitions, and the exact remaining open propositions.
Modify a formal or editorial surface without allowing status or source coordinates to drift.
